Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Data:
- Valid email: user@example.com
- Valid password: Password123!
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email and password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result:
- User is redirected to the home page.
- A success message is displayed.

Title: Unsuccessful User Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User account exists and is active.
Test Data:
- Valid email: user@example.com
- Invalid password: wrongpassword
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the valid email and the incorrect password.
3. Click the login button.
Expected Result:
- User remains on the login page.
- An error message indicating incorrect password is displayed.

Title: User Registration
Preconditions: User does not have an account.
Test Data: - New user details: name, surname, email, password, phone number, DOB, address
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page.
2. Fill in all required fields with valid data.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result:
- User is redirected to the login page.
- A success message indicating successful registration is displayed.

Title: Create Emergency Successfully
Test Data: - Emergency details: name, description, location, start date, end date
Test Steps:
1. Click "Inserisci emergenza".
2. Ensure the coordinator is authenticated.
3. Input all required emergency details.
4. Confirm that the information is complete.
5. Click "Crea emergenza".
Expected Result:
- Emergency is created successfully.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Error Handling for Unauthenticated Coordinator
Test Data: - Emergency details: name, description, location, start date, end date
Test Steps:
1. Click "Inserisci emergenza".
2. Ensure the coordinator is not authenticated.
Expected Result:
- Error message is displayed.
- User is redirected to the login page.

Title: View Emergencies
Test Data: - User is authenticated.
Test Steps:
1. Click "Visualizza emergenze".
2. Check if any emergencies are available.
Expected Result:
- If emergencies are available, they are displayed.
- If no emergencies are available, a message "Nessuna emergenza" is shown.

Title: Remove Volunteer from Task
Test Data: - Emergency and task details.
Test Steps:
1. Click "Visualizza emergenza".
2. Ensure the coordinator is authenticated.
3. Select an emergency.
4. Select a task.
5. Check if a volunteer is assigned to the task.
6. If a volunteer is assigned, remove the volunteer.
Expected Result:
- Volunteer is removed from the task.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Create New Task Successfully
Test Data: - Task details: id, name, description, location, due date
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/nuovoCompito" with valid task details.
2. Verify the token.
3. Insert the task details into the database.
Expected Result:
- Task is created successfully.
- Success message is returned.

Title: Error Handling During Task Creation
Test Data: - Task details: id, name, description, location, due date
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/nuovoCompito" with valid task details.
2. Verify the token.
3. Attempt to insert invalid task details into the database.
Expected Result:
- Error is logged.
- HTTP error (400/401/500) is returned.

Title: Terminate Emergency Successfully
Test Data: - Emergency details: id, name, description, location, start date, end date
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/terminaEmergenza".
2. Verify the token.
3. Update the database to terminate the emergency.
Expected Result:
- Emergency is terminated successfully.
- Message "X emergenza terminata" is returned.

Title: No Emergencies Terminated
Test Data: - Emergency details: id, name, description, location, start date, end date
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/terminaEmergenza".
2. Verify the token.
3. Attempt to terminate an emergency that does not exist.
Expected Result:
- Emergency is not terminated.
- Message "Nessuna emergenza terminata" is returned.

Title: Error Handling for Invalid Token
Test Data: - Emergency details: id, name, description, location, start date, end date
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/terminaEmergenza".
2. Provide an invalid token.
Expected Result:
- Error is logged.
- Process is terminated.
- HTTP error (401) is returned.

- User details: id, name, email
Test Steps: 1. Call the operation "newAssegnazione()" to assign the task to the user.
Expected Result:
- Task is assigned to the user successfully.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Terminate Assignment

Test Steps: 1. Call the operation "terminateAssegnazione()" to terminate the assignment.

- Assignment is terminated successfully.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Delete Assignment
Test Data: - Assignment details: id, task id, user id
Test Steps: 1. Call the operation "deleteAssegnazione()" to delete the assignment.

- Assignment is deleted successfully.
- Confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Generate Token Successfully
Test Data: - User details: id, email, password
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/login" with valid user credentials.
2. Server verifies the user credentials.
3. Server generates a new token.
4. Server updates the tokens table to revoke any existing tokens.
5. Server inserts the new token into the tokens table.
Expected Result:
- Token is generated successfully.
- Token is returned to the client.

Title: Revoke Existing Token
Test Data: - User details: id, email, password
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/login" with valid user credentials.
2. Server verifies the user credentials.
3. Server revokes any existing tokens.
4. Server generates a new token.
5. Server inserts the new token into the tokens table.
Expected Result:
- Existing tokens are revoked successfully.
- New token is generated and returned to the client.

Title: Error Handling for Invalid Credentials
Test Data: - User details: id, email, incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/login" with invalid user credentials.
2. Server verifies the user credentials.
Expected Result:
- Error is logged.
- HTTP error (401) is returned.

Title: Error Handling for Token Generation Failure
Test Data: - User details: id, email, password
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/login" with valid user credentials.
2. Server verifies the user credentials.
3. Server fails to generate a new token.
Expected Result:
- Error is logged.
- HTTP error (500) is returned.

Title: Token Expiration
Test Data: - User details: id, email, password
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to "/login" with valid user credentials.
2. Server verifies the user credentials.
3. Server generates a new token.
4. Wait until the token expires.
5. Attempt to use the expired token.
Expected Result:
- Token is considered expired.
- HTTP error (401) is returned.
